methods.description: To process the pollen samples, we performed an acetolysis method. Acetolysis involves a reaction between acetic anhydride and sulfuric acid. The reaction removes impurities, lipids, and other unwanted substances from the grains, revealing the topography and enhancing important morphological characteristics of the grains. After acetolysis, pollen grains are stained and then observed under a light microscope for identification. In our study, we followed the acetolysis procedure outlined in Jones (2014) and made modifications to optimize the protocol for extremely small pollen samples.

steps.description: we removed the cotton containing the pollen sample from the end of the cotton swab and placed it in a microcentrifuge tube
steps.searchable_summary: Remove cotton swab tip containing pollen sample and place in microcentrifuge tube
outputs: Cotton tip with pollen in microcentrifuge tube

steps.description: We added 1 ml of glacial acetic acid to each sample
steps.searchable_summary: Add glacial acetic acid to sample
inputs: Cotton tip with pollen in microcentrifuge tube
outputs: Sample with glacial acetic acid in microcentrifuge tube

steps.description: centrifuged the samples for 3 min at 1,060 × g
steps.searchable_summary: Centrifuge sample with glacial acetic acid
inputs: Sample with glacial acetic acid in microcentrifuge tube
outputs: Centrifuged sample pellet with acetic acid supernatant

steps.description: We prepared the acetolysis mixture by combining acetic anhydride and sulfuric acid in a 9:1 ratio
We decanted the glacial acetic acid from the tubes
steps.searchable_summary: Decant glacial acetic acid from sample
inputs: Centrifuged sample pellet with acetic acid supernatant

steps.description: added 1 ml of the acetolysis mixture to each tube
steps.searchable_summary: Add acetolysis mixture to sample pellet
outputs: Sample with acetolysis mixture in microcentrifuge tube

steps.description: We stirred each tube with a clean wooden stir stick
inputs: Sample with acetolysis mixture in microcentrifuge tube

steps.description: pushed the cotton to the bottom of the tube
steps.searchable_summary: Push cotton to bottom of tube
outputs: Sample with cotton at bottom of tube

steps.description: added the samples to a preheated water bath (100 °C) for 10 min
steps.searchable_summary: Heat sample in water bath (100°C, 10 min)

steps.description: then centrifuged at 1060 × g for 3 min
steps.searchable_summary: Centrifuge sample after heating and cooling
outputs: Centrifuged sample pellet with acetolysis mixture supernatant

steps.description: We aspirated the acetolysis mixture with a pipette
steps.searchable_summary: Aspirate acetolysis mixture from sample pellet
inputs: Centrifuged sample pellet with acetolysis mixture supernatant

steps.description: added 1 ml of glacial acetic acid to each sample
steps.searchable_summary: Add glacial acetic acid for rinsing
outputs: Sample with glacial acetic acid in microcentrifuge tube

steps.description: We centrifuged the samples for 3 min at 1,060 × g
steps.searchable_summary: Centrifuge sample with glacial acetic acid rinse
inputs: Sample with glacial acetic acid in microcentrifuge tube
outputs: Centrifuged sample pellet with acetic acid supernatant

steps.description: the glacial acetic acid was aspirated
inputs: Centrifuged sample pellet with acetic acid supernatant

steps.description: We performed a water rinse by taking a squirt bottle containing distilled water and forcefully squirting water into the tube until it was threefourths full
steps.searchable_summary: Perform water rinse by squirting distilled water into tube
outputs: Sample with water in microcentrifuge tube

steps.description: Once the samples all had water, they were centrifuged at 1,060 × g for 3 min
inputs: Sample with water in microcentrifuge tube
outputs: Centrifuged sample pellet with water supernatant

steps.searchable_summary: Aspirate water supernatant from sample pellet
inputs: Centrifuged sample pellet with water supernatant

steps.description: We aspirated the water, leaving the pollen ready for staining
steps.searchable_summary: Aspirate remaining water, leaving pollen pellet ready for staining
outputs: Processed pollen pellet ready for staining
